action.reasoning: Observation: local generations from the LoRA checkpoint are valid but biased to a few wrong top-right locations, and eval failed because model.py still resolves the absolute base path /data/base_model/... from meta/default, which is invalid in the isolated eval container. Analysis: score is poor, but we still need a working self-contained eval submission; the checkpoint already includes adapter weights plus copied processor files, so the missing piece is robust base-model resolution inside the checkpoint. The workspace shows checkpoints/baseline/base_model exists, suggesting a packaged local base copy we can reference relatively. Fastest fix is to patch model.py so load_model_for_inference first looks for checkpoint/base_model, then sibling baseline/base_model, then meta.json relative paths, always with local_files_only. Also remove the malformed device_map string bug visible in truncated model.py and test predict.py locally using only the checkpoint path. Decision: make inference self-contained and robust, run a small local predict smoke test from the LoRA checkpoint, then resubmit eval immediately with the best available checkpoint.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/qwen_lora_small --output val_pred_qwen_small_fixed.json --limit 8 && python score_val.py --pred val_pred_qwen_small_fixed.json --gt /data/unified_val.jsonl
